title: Plaintext Credential Storage in LocalStorage
description: Supabase URL, Anon Key, and Gemini API Key are stored in LocalStorage using Base64 'obfuscation'. This is insecure as any XSS vulnerability could lead to full credential theft.

title: Global State Re-rendering and Prop Drilling
description: The root App component manages all global state (issues, projects, session) and drills it down through multiple levels. Any status update to an issue triggers a full re-render of the Dashboard and CodeViewer components.
code_snippet:
const [issues, setIssues] = useState<Issue[]>([]);
const [projects, setProjects] = useState<Project[]>([]);

title: Manual JWT Expiration Check in CLI
description: code-eye.js manually parses and checks JWT expiration. This is brittle and doesn't account for clock skew or server-side revocation.

title: Unsafe Shell Command Execution in Login
description: Opening a URL using cmd.exe /c start with partial escaping is risky if the URL contains shell-sensitive characters not covered by the current regex replacement.

title: Memory Intensive Concurrent File Analysis
description: Uploader.tsx reads all file contents into memory strings before processing them concurrently. This can lead to OOM errors on projects with many large source files.
